name: Cosmic Flow: A Relaxing VR Experience

reviews.review_description: 𝑇ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑎𝑝𝑝 𝑖𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑏𝑎𝑑 𝑏𝑢𝑡 𝑖𝑡'𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑚𝑦 𝑓𝑎𝑣. 𝑒𝑖𝑡ℎ𝑟. 𝐼𝑡'𝑠 𝑔𝑜𝑡 𝑝𝑟𝑒𝑡𝑡𝑦 𝑣𝑖𝑠𝑢𝑎𝑙𝑠 𝑛 𝑑𝑒𝑓 𝑠𝑢𝑚𝑡ℎ𝑛 𝐼 𝑐𝑜𝑢𝑙𝑑 𝑠𝑡𝑎𝑟𝑒 𝑎𝑡 4 ℎ𝑟𝑠, 𝑙𝑜𝑙. 𝑃𝑙𝑧 𝑘𝑒𝑒𝑝 𝑢𝑝𝑑𝑎𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑛 𝑖𝑚𝑝𝑜𝑣𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑠 𝑎𝑝𝑝 𝑖𝑡'𝑠 𝑎 𝑔𝑜𝑜𝑑 𝑎𝑝𝑝 𝑏𝑢𝑡 𝑖𝑡'𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝐸𝑣𝑟𝑦𝑤𝑎𝑦 𝑉𝑖𝑠𝑢𝑎𝑙𝑖𝑠𝑒𝑟 𝑎𝑝𝑝.

reviews.review_title: Great BUT.......The ring in my vision.
reviews.review_description: The experience is great but what is the purpose of the ring that won't go away during the experience?  You know the one I'm talking about it's the ring that's dead center and follows your eyes the entire time.  There's no purpose for it and it's very distracting and annoying 👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻👎🏻  PLEASE GET RID OF THE RING 🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻🙏🏻

reviews.review_title: the application's name speaks for itself
reviews.review_description: this is great when listening to an audio book or just wanting to take a time out this is so visually soothing. well done to the application developers on this.
The most peaceful and relaxing thing I've found in VR so far. Highly recommended.

I really love this app, I like to work out while standing and the nice thing about this app is that every direction you look there is beauty. It’s also interesting to get off center of the direction that the flow is going to see the patterns from the side. I enjoy being included music, but I often listen to other music on the home system or headphones the more I use it the more I like it I will sometimes have it on for a half hour while listening to music.
Very relaxing. Thank you for making this free!
This is a very good app for free. Super good way to meditate.
Stunning visuals and a great app to drift off to. Highly recommended if you like this kinda stuff.

This experience is incredible. To the visual artist who designed this, I express my gratitude. Extremely psychedelic even if you aren’t on drugs. It may put you in an altered state when you lie down and get immersed in the visuals.

reviews.review_title: Best ever! Make some more, Medved!
reviews.review_description: This is 100% what I wanted from VR. It's trippy in the best possible way, relaxing, with so many parameters.  I wish you could decide the colors - but then again, nope! I'd always pick the same thing. But I wish he had a couple more of these apps - it's in my top 2! He did a favor to mankind by making something to make it easier for those of us who can't meditation with their eyes closed. This will conquer monkeymind, I promise.

This app is phenomenal! It is an amazing display of the beauty of color and kaliedoscopes, such a peaceful and tranquil way to press the pause button on life and just take in a visual feast of colors and designs! There needs to be more apps like this! And the best part is it's free! I hope the developer adds more experiences to this app, like different types of kaliedascopes or different music tracks, but the app is breath takingly beautiful as it is, if your a visual person like me and you love colors DOWNLOAD THIS APP IMMEDIATELY!!!
Beautifully immersive visuals that make for such a relaxing experience
Please make more like it!

Looks so beautiful on the quest 3

Love showing this to others
Absolutely ethereal and breathtaking visuals with incredible resolution, even for quest 3

Cosmic Flow is a skillfully crafted meditation app that only does one thing but does it very well, placing you inside a 360 degree visual abstraction that gently explodes and drifts around you.

A kaleidoscope of ethereal strands and small particles radiate out of a distant point, gradually cycling through multicolored permutations accompanied by a relaxing ambient soundtrack. Settings allow you to adjust the speed of the experience, your distance from the point of radiance and the intensity of the particles that surround you. It's also easy to reset your view in the headset, including any reclining angle and lying flat.

This is one of our favorite virtual reality meditation experiences. Possibly it could have benefited from a more diverse cycle of shapes and colors to increase the visual stimuli, though it's also possible that that could distract the viewer from this captivating experience.

The soundtrack is very pleasant, and of course you can also mute this in your device and use headphones to provide your own alternative soundscape.

We found three versions of Cosmic Flow offering slightly differing experiences. The Quest app and Viveport PCVR version have similar visuals, though the Quest version also includes an alternative soundtrack and the ability to draw some particles (called "tiny galaxies") around you. For the life of us we couldn't find a way to open up the settings in the Steam PCVR version so assume that's not available, also that PCVR version has a noticeably different visual pattern to the experience.

✅ Simple but captivating meditative experience.
❌ May have benefited from a more diverse range of visuals.
Love this felt so trippy def a must try

As a high school senior it's been stressful and tiring. Opened this app I've felt at peace and relaxed. Being able to control everything in this app, including creating galaxies with your controller, while you're mind is at peace feels breath taking, and it's all FREE!!! I highly recommend this for people that's stressed!

This is the best app I've seen on Quest 2 for zoning out and relaxing!  
I immediately am fascinated and almost hypnotized by watching the shapes and colors.
The fact that it is free is also wonderful. 
Thanks so much!
Period.

this is such an effective form of meditation and is accessible in a simple way- one click to open the app and you're into the experience immediately. you can alter speed and pattern width settings using the overlay, mid-experience, and use it to your heart's content (it's an endless cosmic flow)!

reviews.review_title: One thing needs to be improved...
reviews.review_description:
This is now my favorite form of meditation. 

The experience is similar to staring at a candle flame in a dark room. There is a central focus point from which a trippy kaleidoscopic tunnel emanates with pleasing and relaxing colors and shapes. The exact center of focus is totally customizable with your controllers meaning you can find the most comfortable sitting position first and then adjust the app to place the focus where it naturally should be. I can't over emphasize how important this detail is.

The advantage is that you don't need a dark room or a candle or the need to prepare a proper sitting environment at the right height to watch the flame, etc. You just need to slap on your headset and find a comfortable position on your couch, preferably with your head supported.

The single thing that MUST be improved is how the experience ends. You may set this app to play for any length of time you wish, but when that 10 minutes is up, the images and music comes to a very abrupt end which is incredibly jarring and detracts from the experience immensely.

A simple fade-to-black with an audio fade at the end of the time limit would dramatically improve this app and I feel the creators must already been working on this code. This would literally make this app 100% perfect for me.

In addition, while the ambient music is pretty good, it would be a huge improvement to have 5-10 versions of music to choose from. The current music is a definite improvement over the original music that is also an option.

Thank you for this app and I look forward to future improvements.

As someone who suffers from anxiety I was curious to try Cosmic Flow out - initially on SideQuest - and I'm so glad this has made it to the App Lab as it deserves that.

What you have here is a kaleidoscopic series of colours and shapes moving around in space, along with some ambient audio tracks which may or may not be randomly generated. Think of sounds that can be created by a program called Mixtikl (a program on the Apple Store which generates tracks) and you get the idea.

The whole concept is so therapeutic and relaxing that I actually fell asleep for around an hour listening to the tunes while lying on my back. No program has ever done this to me before, and I could completely empty my mind of any worries or stresses that I had. It's a very sensory experience and very therapeutic.

I would absolutely recommend that everyone gives this a try.
PPLLEEAASSEE make the app hands-free!  Finding my controllers isn't necessary for this app it seems, but also prevents certain implementations...hoping you'll consider!

Of all the relaxation apps I have tried, this is by far the best. I have been using it since I got my Quest 2 a year ago. Got on SideQuest before it was on App Lab site. Very ethereal. Puts me in a very relaxed state of mind. Kudos to the developer.
This is a modern day version of a kaleidoscope.  Pretty but boring after a few seconds.

The visuals and audio are both excellent. There a few settings you can change which is nice, but it would be even cooler if there were more settings you could change like more patterns and music/sounds to choose from. Overall it is a well done app, especially considering it’s free.

reviews.review_title: Add some Magic to your VR experience
reviews.review_description:
Absolutely incredible! A visual trip that goes hand in hand with life's real Magic Trip's.
The visuals constantly change and never get boring. I personally put headphones over my headset and played my own music while vibing out. I suggest laying on your bed while enjoying the ride.

Very relaxing experience with wonderful visuals - looks more like Pcvr, really technically brilliant on the mobile Quest.

Can sink some time in, it's like staring at a campfire ;-)

Somehow it would be cool if you could slowly navigate within this cosmic wonder ;-)

Visuals and sounds are amazing
I use it everyday
Love it

Recline yourself somewhere comfortable and check this app out.  Don’t mess with any settings or options at first, just go with the default and hit start.  Keep your visual focus at the center.  Let your eyes adjust to that and as the periphery blurs a bit, as will happen when you focus on the same spot, the effect is fantastic.  Give it 10 minutes.  I cannot imagine anyone not being impressed with this VR experience.
  After you do that, start tweaking the settings!  Speed, rotation, depth, ambient particles, can all be adjusted.  Doing so can create some mind-blowing visuals.
  There are two ambient music tracks to select from.  Both are great and work well with the visuals.  Some have mentioned playing your own music (headphones or however) and that’s a great idea.  The app music can be turned off.
  First time I used this app I ran the battery dead on the headset from a full charge.  Still happens all the time.  A real visual treat, and probably more appreciated by the adult gamer.      Just relax and watch it.
Highest recommendation!
For me it is the best application that exists to relax and meditate.  I love it.   It is fabulous.
Cosmic Flow is so good! I use it with “laying down mode” on my Quest 3 and it’s so relaxing. Just feel like I’m floating through space with beautiful colors and patterns. Download and enjoy!!
This is so relaxing.

A big thanks to the developers, this is something we also need in VR. Just something to float away. The options you have to change the experience is wonderful. Graphics are insane. Very good work. Like it a lot. Thanks again. 👍
Simple, beautiful, mesmerizing. I enjoy just zoning out in this app when I don’t want any guidance and I’m not sure how long I want to meditate.

It has the option to use it laying down.
Great psychedelic experience.
It’s very relaxing
Is great for what it is - and potential to be so much more.

'Purchased' app a few days ago, but it does not show up onto my Quest 3. It keeps saying purchased here, but doesnt show up anywhere else... I havent had this issue with any other App from this store.

developer_response.body: Sorry you're having issues, it sounds like a Meta Quest store problem, contacting their support may help.
